title: Probability Theory
description: Master the laws of probability, including independent events, conditional probability, and Bayes' Theorem.

contentBlocks.text: Probability measures the likelihood of an event occurring. For any event $A$, $0 \le P(A) \le 1$. The complement rule states that $P(A') = 1 - P(A)$.
The probability of event $A$ occurring given that $B$ has already occurred is: \n\n$$P(A|B) = \frac{P(A \cap B)}{P(B)}$$
